sections.heading: The big idea
sections.body: **Dynamic Programming** is recursion plus memory. If you'd compute the same subproblem twice, save its answer and look it up next time. That turns exponential time into polynomial time. It's the most feared pattern in interviews and the most satisfying when it clicks.

sections.title: Keep a notebook
sections.body: First time someone asks 'what's fib(10)?', you compute it the long way and write it down. Next time, you just read the notebook. Same idea — every DP solution is a really thorough notebook.

sections.prompt: Write `climbStairs(n)` — number of distinct ways to climb a staircase taking 1 or 2 steps at a time.
hints: Ways to reach step n = ways to reach (n-1) + ways to reach (n-2).
